description: News aggregator site hosted in Azure that tracks information on layoffs and surrounding fields such as resumes, interview tips, and career advice

updateSchedule.method: Separate query runs to update feeds
updateSchedule.whatUpdates: News and video feeds are refreshed

keyPages.name: Homepage
keyPages.description: Main feed showing current layoff news and videos
importantElements: Topic filters: Interview Tips, Resume Writing, ATS, Layoffs, AI, Job Search, Career Advice, Unemployment

keyPages.name: Current
keyPages.description: Small current view of latest items

keyPages.name: Analytics
keyPages.description: AI-generated interesting information about articles - changes often
keyPages.note: Content is AI-generated and changes often - test structure, not specific content

testThese: Content type filter buttons (All, News, Video) functionality
Topic filter buttons (Interview Tips, Resume Writing, ATS, etc.) functionality
Current page shows small current view
Analytics page structure (sections exist, not specific content)
dontTestThese: Specific article titles or headlines (change with updates)
Exact trending topic names in Analytics (AI-generated, changes often)
Specific company names in Analytics (AI-generated, changes often)
Exact numbers in Analytics (AI-generated, changes often)
testingGuidance.priority: Test functionality, filters, and structure. Do NOT test specific content that changes with scheduled updates or AI generation.
specialNotes: Analytics page content is AI-generated and changes often - only test that sections exist and structure is correct
Feeds update twice daily at 7am and 7pm - content will vary between runs
Archive shows last 500 items - exact items will change
Current shows small current view - exact items will change

knownBehaviors: Content updates twice daily at 7:00 AM and 7:00 PM via separate query
News and video feeds are aggregated from multiple sources
Filter buttons update content client-side (React SPA)
Topic filters allow filtering by category (Interview Tips, Resume Writing, ATS, etc.)
Content type filters allow filtering by News or Video
Current page shows a small current view of latest items
Archive page shows the last 500 items
Analytics page content is AI-generated and changes frequently
Site is a React single-page application
No user authentication required - all content public
External article links may open in new tabs

articleFeed.whatChanges: Article titles, sources, publication dates, content, video titles
articleFeed.whatStaysSame: Feed structure, filter buttons, page layout, navigation, topic filters

analyticsPage.whatChanges: Trending topics, company names, statistics, insights, charts data
analyticsPage.whatStaysSame: Page structure, section layout, chart types, navigation

archivePage.whatChanges: Items in the last 500 list, article titles, dates

steps: View latest items in small current view

userWorkflows.name: View Analytics

steps: Note: Content is AI-generated and changes often
userWorkflows.note: Test structure only, not specific content

contentTypeFilters.All: Shows both news and video content

filterBehavior.filterInteraction: Filters update content client-side without page reload (React SPA)
filterBehavior.filterCombinations: Content type and topic filters can be combined

customTestCases.name: Analytics Top Companies Filter Count Verification
customTestCases.description: On the analytics page, the 'Top Companies Mentioned' section contains clickable filters. Each filter shows a company name with a mention count (e.g., 'Apple 8 mentions' or 'Microsoft 5 mentions'). When a filter is clicked, the number of articles displayed should match the count shown on the filter. For example, if a filter says 'Apple 8 mentions', clicking it should display exactly 8 articles.

_customTestCasesNote: Custom test cases allow you to specify test scenarios in plain English. The AI will convert these descriptions into structured test steps. Each test case should have: name (required), description (required, plain English), page (optional, URL path), and priority (optional, 'high', 'medium', or 'low').
